html:
The Feather Hat is a Hat Item found in the Dress Tab. It requires 2 Crimson Feathers, 3 Jet Feathers and 2 Tentacle Spots to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype. A Sewing Kit repairs 62.5% of its durability.

It restores 2 Sanity/min while worn.

When worn, the Feather Hat attracts more flying Birds (Redbirds, Snowbirds and Crows) to land on the ground. By default, only 4 Birds at most will land with a delay of 5–15 seconds in between. With the Feather Hat equipped, up to 7 Birds may land with a 2-10 second delay. Birds will still fly away from a character wearing this headdress if the character approaches them.

The Feather Hat can be given to a Pig or Bunnyman to wear, which provides no effect. It will still lose durability while worn.

Downloadable Content

In the Hamlet DLC, Feather Hats can be bought for 5 Oincs at The Sow's Ear Hat Shop in the Pig City.

Tips

  • If trying to summon Krampus by killing birds without Wickerbottom and her Books, one can equip this hat while standing on Grass Turf to make a lot of birds spawn, then kill them with a Boomerang or another Ranged Weapon as they land on the ground.
  • In the Reign of Giants DLC, the player can also farm birds by trapping a Catcoon in a 1x1 cage with no corners and wearing the Feather Hat. The Catcoon will kill the birds that land on the ground.

Trivia

  • The Feather Hat was added in the Enter the Gobbler update.
  • The Feather Hat used to cost 100 Research points to craft, and it had a value of 10 Science Points when used on a Science Machine.
  • Unlike most crafted items, one can actually see what each individual ingredient was used for. The two Crimson Feathers are in between the three Jet Feathers, and the Tentacle Spots are used for the headband. While the hat's side view seems to imply that there may be a total of four Crimson Feathers, with only two visible from the front and back, this may be unintentional.
  • Although the Feather Hat is included in the crafting menu of Hamlet worlds, there are no sources of Tentacle Spots in Hamlet, making it uncraftable without using the Skyworthy or buying at The Sow's Ear Hat Shop.

Gallery

  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Feather Hat Equipped

  • Willow wearing a Feather Hat.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Feather Hat.

  • The Feather Hat attracting Redbirds.

"featherhat"
“I AM A BIRD!”
–Wilson
“I am a phoenix!”
–Willow
“Is silly hat of feather. Is Wolfgang bird now?”
–Wolfgang
“Ca-caw!”
–Wendy
“I STILL CAN NOT FLY”
–WX-78
“Not quite the genuine article, but I might fool some birds.”
–Wickerbottom
“I want nothing to do with that... thing.”
–Woodie
“I will lure them in with my plumage.”
–Maxwell
“Attracting birds. Perhaps they think me one of them?”
–Wagstaff
“Seems a bit flashy för battle.”
–Wigfrid
“Feathers enough to cover our spiderness.”
–Webber
“I'm at one with my feathered friends.”
–Walani
“What am I supposed to do with this?”
–Warly
“'Tis a hat o'birdy treasures.”
–Woodlegs
“WILBA IS'T BIRD NOW. TWEET TWEET”
–Wilba
“Tweeter disguise”
–Wormwood
“All these feathers and no wings.”
–Wheeler
“Well la-dee-da.”
–Winona
“Feathers and horns go well together.”
–Wortox
“Look like bird, think like bird.”
–Wurt
“And I thought my bird watching badge was a feather in my cap!”
–Walter
“Oooh, how eclectic!”
–Wanda
The Feather Hat is a Hat Item found in the Dress Tab. It requires 2 Crimson Feathers, 3 Jet Feathers and 2 Tentacle Spots to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype. A Sewing Kit repairs 62.5% of its durability.
It restores 2 Sanity/min while worn.
When worn, the Feather Hat attracts more flying Birds (Redbirds, Snowbirds and Crows) to land on the ground. By default, only 4 Birds at most will land with a delay of 5–15 seconds in between. With the Feather Hat equipped, up to 7 Birds may land with a 2-10 second delay. Birds will still fly away from a character wearing this headdress if the character approaches them.
The Feather Hat can be given to a Pig or Bunnyman to wear, which provides no effect. It will still lose durability while worn.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Hamlet DLC, Feather Hats can be bought for 5 Oincs at The Sow's Ear Hat Shop in the Pig City.
Tips[edit | edit source]
If trying to summon Krampus by killing birds without Wickerbottom and her Books, one can equip this hat while standing on Grass Turf to make a lot of birds spawn, then kill them with a Boomerang or another Ranged Weapon as they land on the ground.
In the Reign of Giants DLC, the player can also farm birds by trapping a Catcoon in a 1x1 cage with no corners and wearing the Feather Hat. The Catcoon will kill the birds that land on the ground.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Feather Hat was added in the Enter the Gobbler update.
The Feather Hat used to cost 100 Research points to craft, and it had a value of 10 Science Points when used on a Science Machine.
Unlike most crafted items, one can actually see what each individual ingredient was used for. The two Crimson Feathers are in between the three Jet Feathers, and the Tentacle Spots are used for the headband. While the hat's side view seems to imply that there may be a total of four Crimson Feathers, with only two visible from the front and back, this may be unintentional.
Although the Feather Hat is included in the crafting menu of Hamlet worlds, there are no sources of Tentacle Spots in Hamlet, making it uncraftable without using the Skyworthy or buying at The Sow's Ear Hat Shop.

It requires 2 [[Crimson Feather]]s, 3 [[Jet Feather]]s and 2 [[Tentacle Spots]] to craft and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. A [[Sewing Kit]] repairs 62.5% of its durability. It restores 2 [[Sanity]]/min while worn. When worn, the Feather Hat attracts more flying [[Birds]] ([[Redbird]]s, [[Snowbird]]s and [[Crow]]s) to land on the ground. By default, only 4 Birds at most will land with a delay of 5–15 seconds in between. With the Feather Hat equipped, up to 7 Birds may land with a 2-10 second delay. Birds will still fly away from a character wearing this headdress if the character approaches them. The Feather Hat can be given to a [[Pig]] or [[Bunnyman]] to wear, which provides no effect. It will still lose durability while worn. __NOTOC__ == {{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content == In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Feather Hats can be bought for 5 [[Oinc]]s at [[The Sow's Ear Hat Shop]] in the [[Pig City]]. == {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * If trying to summon [[Krampus]] by killing birds without [[Wickerbottom]] and her [[Books]], one can equip this hat while standing on [[Grass Turf]] to make a lot of birds spawn, then kill them with a [[Boomerang]] or another [[Weapon#Ranged weapons|Ranged Weapon]] as they land on the ground. * In the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, the player can also farm birds by trapping a [[Catcoon]] in a 1x1 cage with no corners and wearing the Feather Hat. The Catcoon will kill the birds that land on the ground. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Feather Hat was added in the ''[[Version History#November 13.2C 2012 .E2.80.93 Enter the Gobbler.5B17.5D|Enter the Gobbler]]'' update. * The Feather Hat used to cost 100 [[Research points]] to craft, and it had a value of 10 Science Points when used on a [[Science Machine]]. * Unlike most crafted items, one can actually see what each individual ingredient was used for. The two Crimson Feathers are in between the three Jet Feathers, and the Tentacle Spots are used for the headband. While the hat's side view seems to imply that there may be a total of four Crimson Feathers, with only two visible from the front and back, this may be unintentional. * Although the Feather Hat is included in the crafting menu of ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' worlds, there are no sources of Tentacle Spots in ''Hamlet'', making it uncraftable without using the [[Skyworthy]] or buying at [[The Sow's Ear Hat Shop]]. {{clear}} == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == CD featherhat.png|Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from [[CD Don't Starve]].
